title: amaryllis × recurvata
intro: Amaryllis × recurvata, also known as the Belladonna Lily, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family. Its roots and bulbs are used in traditional medicine, particularly in African and Asian cultures, for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Amaryllis × recurvata include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to act as a natural pain reliever and anticonvulsant. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and bulbs can be dried and used to make teas, tinctures, or infusions, although it is essential to note that its use should be under the guidance of a medical professional due to its potential toxicity.
